summary: A fair‑quality audience tape of a mid‑90s Dead show that delivers magical moments (So Many Roads, Hell In A Bucket, Drums→Space) despite uneven playing and occasional audio hiccups.
review: The performance is an average slice of the 1994 era – first set solid, second set a bit ragged with the Victim‑>Eyes segment noted as especially out‑of‑tune – but the crowd energy and several standout tunes (So Many Roads, Hell In A Bucket, Drums→Space, Brokedown Palace) give it lasting charm.  The recording sounds like an audience capture; it is clear enough to enjoy the highlights but suffers from muddiness and rain‑induced noise in places.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show around average (3 ★) with a few strong positives (5 ★) and negatives (2 ★).  The mix of performance praise and audio complaints leads to a moderate overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners report occasional muddiness, rain‑affected sections, and an unlistenable second‑set segment, but also note crisp details on songs like So Many Roads.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set generally solid; second set uneven with a weak Victim‑>Eyes stretch, but the overall vibe was high thanks to crowd energy and several memorable jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as both magical (singing directly to the reviewer) and distant/tired in parts; some feel he preferred Grisman that night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned as bringing upbeat momentum while Jerry drifted slower, creating a perceived ‘battle’ between them.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums→Space segment praised as a highlight of the show.
